what_it_does: Implements conversation summarization for an episodic-memory system, routing short or Codex-origin conversations to either the Claude Agent SDK or a Codex CLI app-server subprocess, with reentrancy guards, session resume, model fallback, and hierarchical chunking for long conversations.
observations: Supports two summarization backends: direct Claude Agent SDK query() and Codex CLI app-server JSON-RPC-ish protocol.
Sets EPISODIC_MEMORY_SUMMARIZER_GUARD in getApiEnv() and checks it via shouldSkipReentrantSync() to break recursive sync loops.
Resume sessions require the original cwd to still exist on disk; otherwise it falls back to a fresh prompt with full conversation text.
callClaude retries with a fallback model when the result contains a thinking.budget_tokens API error.
Hierarchical summarization splits conversations longer than 15 exchanges into chunks of 8, summarizes each chunk, then synthesizes a final summary.
Trivial conversations (empty, single very short, or /exit) return a fixed trivial string.
extractSummary expects <summary>...</summary> XML tags and falls back to trimmed text.
Codex summarization forks an ephemeral read-only thread and starts a turn with a strict prompt.
getCodexModel deliberately ignores historical model ids to avoid subscription/API-key mismatches.
runCodexCommand uses SIGTERM cleanup and an idempotent finish() closure to avoid double resolution.
standouts: Dual-backend routing between Claude SDK and Codex CLI app-server in one file.
Reentrancy guard env var to prevent recursive summarizer-spawned sync cycles.
Resume-fallback logic tied to cwd existence and SDK error subtype.
Explicit prompt engineering with XML output constraints and good/bad examples.
conventions: Exported functions for testability alongside private closure helpers.
TypeScript interfaces for command and request shapes.
Regex-based XML extraction for structured LLM output.
Child-process spawn with stdio pipes and line-by-line JSON parsing.
Idempotent completion closure pattern in async subprocess drivers.
concerns: readCommandOutput concatenates stdout and stderr into one string, which can obscure error context.
callClaude returns an empty string if the SDK iterator yields no 'result' message, rather than failing explicitly.
Multiple any casts around SDK message shapes weaken type safety.
If the fallback model also hits a thinking budget error, the raw API error string is returned as the summary.
Hierarchical chunk summarization never passes sessionId, so resume context is unavailable for chunks.
runCodexCommand sends SIGTERM but does not wait for process exit, potentially leaving zombies.
assertSupportedCodexVersion depends on parsing --version output whose format is not defined here.
notes: This file is the summarization engine: it decides which backend to use, guards against reentrant loops, and can chunk long conversations.
questions: What are the exact fields of ConversationExchange and what values can harness take?
What string does SUMMARIZER_CONTEXT_MARKER expand to and where is it defined?
What version string format does parseCodexCliVersion expect and what minimum is required?
Does the Claude Agent SDK query() iterator ever yield more than one result message per call?
What is the full app-server protocol contract for thread/fork, turn/start, and item/completed?
purpose_vs_actual: The file's stated purpose (summarize conversations) matches its behavior: it routes to backends, handles resume/fallback, and chunks long inputs.
apparent_themes: Reentrancy safety and recursive loop prevention
Graceful degradation through fallback models and backends
Session lifecycle management (resume, fork, ephemeral turns)
intention_signals: The code is trying to make summarization reliable across different conversation sources and lengths while avoiding infinite recursion when the summarizer itself runs inside a Claude Agent SDK session.
novelty: Combines Claude SDK direct calls with Codex CLI app-server forked-thread summarization in a single backend-agnostic entry point, plus an env-var reentrancy guard.
contribution: Provides a backend-routing summarizer that keeps both Claude SDK and Codex CLI paths, preserves reentrancy guard behavior, resume/fallback handling, hierarchical chunking, and strict prompt templates for a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: A TypeScript summarizer module that uses the Claude Agent SDK or a Codex CLI app-server subprocess to produce XML-tagged conversation summaries, with reentrancy guards, session resume, model fallback, and hierarchical chunking.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to generate concise, factual summaries of recorded conversations for later semantic retrieval while preventing recursive summarizer invocation and supporting both Claude and Codex execution contexts.

symbols.does: Typed error for SDK result messages marked is_error.
symbols.description: Custom Error subclass carrying the SDK failure subtype and optional session_id so callers can dispatch structurally instead of parsing strings.

symbols.does: Checks whether a SummarizerSdkError signals a resume execution failure.
symbols.description: Returns true only when the error is a SummarizerSdkError with subtype 'error_during_execution', used to trigger non-resume fallback.

symbols.does: Describes a Codex CLI invocation for summarization.
symbols.description: Interface bundling the binary path, args, prompt, target session id, optional model, version args, and version-check skip flag.

symbols.does: Builds environment overrides for summarization API/subprocess calls.
symbols.description: Merges process.env with optional custom base URL, token, timeout, and always sets EPISODIC_MEMORY_SUMMARIZER_GUARD to prevent reentrant sync loops.

symbols.does: Detects whether the current process is inside a summarizer-spawned SDK subprocess.
symbols.description: Reads EPISODIC_MEMORY_SUMMARIZER_GUARD; sync entry points use it to bail before re-entering the sync->summarizer->spawn cycle.

symbols.does: Renders exchanges as alternating User/Agent text.
symbols.description: Maps each ConversationExchange to 'User: ...\n\nAgent: ...' and joins chunks with '---' separators.

symbols.does: Pulls content from a <summary> XML tag or returns trimmed text.
symbols.description: Uses a regex to extract the first <summary>...</summary> block; if absent, falls back to the entire trimmed string.

symbols.does: Constructs the options object for the Claude Agent SDK query() call.
symbols.description: Sets model, max_tokens, env, resume session id, persistSession false, cwd if it exists, and a system prompt only for fresh sessions.

symbols.does: Returns the prompt for a Codex forked-session summary.
symbols.description: Instructs the ephemeral Codex fork to use only available session context, avoid tool use, and output a single <summary> block in 2-4 sentences.

symbols.does: Builds a CodexSummarizerCommand for app-server mode.
symbols.description: Selects the codex binary from argument, env, or PATH default and returns a command object with args ['app-server'] and the provided prompt/session/model.

symbols.does: Calls the Claude Agent SDK query() to summarize and handles model fallback.
symbols.description: Iterates SDK messages, throws on is_error results, retries with the fallback model on thinking.budget_tokens API errors, and returns the result string.

symbols.does: Represents an in-flight JSON-RPC-style request to the Codex app-server.
symbols.description: Interface storing the request method plus resolve/reject callbacks so responses arriving on stdout can be matched by id.

symbols.does: Reads the Codex app-server summary timeout in milliseconds.
symbols.description: Parses EPISODIC_MEMORY_CODEX_SUMMARY_TIMEOUT_MS, defaulting to 120000 ms when missing or invalid.

symbols.does: Runs a command and captures combined stdout and stderr.
symbols.description: Spawns a child process with getApiEnv(), concatenates stdout and stderr, and resolves with the output on exit code 0 or rejects otherwise.

symbols.does: Validates the Codex CLI version meets requirements.
symbols.description: Runs the binary with --version (or provided versionArgs), parses the version, and throws a requirement message if it is unsupported.

symbols.does: Extracts a thread id from an app-server result.
symbols.description: Validates that result.thread.id is a non-empty string, throwing a descriptive error with the raw response otherwise.

symbols.does: Extracts a turn id from an app-server result.
symbols.description: Validates that result.turn.id is a non-empty string, throwing a descriptive error with the raw response otherwise.

symbols.does: Executes a Codex app-server subprocess to fork a session and generate a summary.
symbols.description: Spawns codex app-server, drives initialize/fork/turn-start via JSON-RPC-ish stdin/stdout, aggregates message deltas, and resolves the final answer or rejects on timeout/error/exit.

symbols.does: Closes the app-server line reader and terminates the child process.
symbols.description: Internal closure that clears the timeout, closes the readline interface, and sends SIGTERM to the codex child if still alive.

symbols.does: Idempotently resolves or rejects the runCodexCommand promise.
symbols.description: Internal closure that guards against double completion, invokes cleanup, and then either rejects with an error or resolves with the summary result.

symbols.does: Sends a notification (no id) to the app-server stdin.
symbols.description: Internal closure that writes a JSON object with method and optional params followed by a newline.

symbols.does: Wraps building and running a Codex summarizer command.
symbols.description: Constructs a CodexSummarizerCommand from the prompt, session id, and optional model, then delegates to runCodexCommand.

symbols.does: Splits a conversation exchange list into fixed-size chunks.
symbols.description: Slices the exchanges array into sub-arrays of the requested chunk size for hierarchical summarization.

symbols.does: Determines whether to use the Codex backend and which session id.
symbols.description: Returns the provided session id or the first exchange's sessionId only when at least one exchange has harness 'codex'; otherwise undefined.

symbols.does: Selects the model for Codex thread/fork.
symbols.description: Ignores historical exchange models to avoid API-key/subscription mismatches and returns EPISODIC_MEMORY_CODEX_MODEL if set, otherwise undefined so app-server uses its configured default.

symbols.does: Entry point that summarizes a conversation via Codex or Claude with hierarchical fallback.
symbols.description: Handles trivial conversations, tries Codex fork summarization for codex harness sessions, falls back to direct Claude SDK summarization for short conversations (with resume and retry), and uses hierarchical chunking for long conversations.
